corpus_scope.corpus_identity: immutable 15-document E2E corpus used by the frozen retrieval suite
corpus_scope.corpus_id_policy: discover from frozen-run artifacts and verify exact 15-title selection at run time

scoring.pass_per_query: at least one expected title in the first 3 routed documents and no forbidden title at rank 1

forbidden_winner_selection_audit.rule: enumerate every lens, optics, or camera-language-only title in the immutable 15-document selection
titles: Blain Brown - Cinematography - Theory and Practice (2016).md

queries.question: How should I prompt an AI video model so camera movement expresses the story instead of feeling like random motion?
expected_title_any: Michael Rabiger, Mick Hurbis-Cherrier - Directing - Film Techniques and Aesthetics (2020).md

queries.question: What underlying craft should guide prompts that turn separate AI-generated clips into one emotionally coherent sequence?
expected_title_any: Walter Murch - In the Blink of an Eye (2001).md

queries.question: How can I describe staging and blocking in an AI video prompt so characters reveal intention through the scene?
expected_title_any: Michael Rabiger, Mick Hurbis-Cherrier - Directing - Film Techniques and Aesthetics (2020).md

queries.question: Which storytelling principles should shape an AI video prompt when I want the frame to communicate power and vulnerability?
expected_title_any: Michael Rabiger, Mick Hurbis-Cherrier - Directing - Film Techniques and Aesthetics (2020).md

queries.question: My AI video character moves correctly but the performance says nothing. What directing craft should I encode in the prompt?
expected_title_any: Michael Rabiger, Mick Hurbis-Cherrier - Directing - Film Techniques and Aesthetics (2020).md
Richard Williams - The Animator's Survival Kit (2002, Faber & Faber).md

queries.question: How do I turn a narrative beat into a sequence of AI video prompts without losing the dramatic through-line?
expected_title_any: Michael Rabiger, Mick Hurbis-Cherrier - Directing - Film Techniques and Aesthetics (2020).md
